title: The Story of My Experiments with Truth
description: The Story of My Experiments with Truth — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "The Story of My Experiments with Truth" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: Abdulla Sheth
memes.description: A wealthy Memon merchant in Durban and partner of Dada Abdulla & Co., whose commercial dispute brought Gandhi to South Africa and who became his first host and early patron in Natal.

memes.label: Ahimsa
memes.description: The principle of nonviolence and non-harming toward all living beings, the foundational ethical commitment underlying Gandhi's satyagraha

memes.label: Ahmedabad
memes.description: Major city in Gujarat — home to the Sabarmati Ashram and the site of Gandhi's 1918 mill-workers' strike, one of his most significant labor interventions

memes.label: Ahmedabad Mill Strike
memes.description: Gandhi's 1918 intervention in a dispute between Ahmedabad textile workers and mill owners — the occasion of his first public fast in India

memes.label: Albert West
memes.description: British printer and colleague of Gandhi in South Africa who managed Indian Opinion during Gandhi's absences

memes.label: All-India Spinners' Association
memes.description: The organization founded to promote hand-spinning and khadi as part of Gandhi's constructive programme for economic self-reliance

memes.label: Allahabad
memes.description: City in Uttar Pradesh at the confluence of the Ganges and Yamuna — where Gandhi missed his train en route to Bombay and later where noncooperation deliberations extended through the night

memes.label: Amritsar
memes.description: City in Punjab where Gandhi was arrested in 1919 during the Rowlatt agitation, and site of the Jallianwala Bagh massacre shortly thereafter.

memes.label: Annie Besant
memes.description: British theosophist, women's rights activist, and Home Rule leader in India who was among the prominent public figures Gandhi respected and consulted during his early years back in India

memes.label: Balasundaram
memes.description: A Tamil indentured labourer in South Africa whose brutal beating by his European master led Gandhi to his first direct intervention on behalf of the poorest Indians — a transformative moment that shaped his commitment to serving the oppressed.

memes.label: Baroda
memes.description: Princely state in Gujarat where the Ganganath Vidyalaya school operated and where Gandhi later found the spinning wheel for his khadi movement.

memes.label: Benares
memes.description: Ancient city on the Ganges (now Varanasi) — visited by Gandhi during his third-class India tour, where he witnessed the exploitation of pilgrims by priests and pandas

memes.label: Bettiah
memes.description: A town in the Champaran district of Bihar where Gandhi stayed during the 1917 Champaran Satyagraha, near Rajkumar Shukla's home, and where his engagement with cow-protection work began through the local Marwadi community.

memes.label: Bhagavad Gita
memes.description: Ancient Hindu scripture that served as Gandhi's spiritual guide and primary source of philosophical inspiration throughout his life.

memes.label: Bihar
memes.description: Eastern Indian province where Gandhi conducted the Champaran Satyagraha of 1917, his first major civil disobedience campaign in India.

memes.label: Black Plague Johannesburg
memes.description: The 1904 outbreak of pneumonic plague in a Johannesburg gold mine, where Gandhi organized an emergency response for the affected Indian and African workers and mobilized the Indian community in relief efforts.

memes.label: Boer War
memes.description: The 1899–1902 conflict in South Africa during which Gandhi organized an Indian ambulance corps — his first experience of wartime humanitarian service

memes.label: Bombay
memes.description: Major Indian port city where Gandhi established his legal practice after returning from England and from South Africa — a key center of political and business support

memes.label: Brahmacharya
memes.description: The vow of celibacy and self-restraint that Gandhi considered essential preparation for the practice of satyagraha and the realization of truth

memes.label: Bread Labor
memes.description: The principle, derived from Ruskin and Tolstoy, that every person must perform physical labor to earn their living — a discipline Gandhi made mandatory in his ashrams

memes.label: Brighton
memes.description: Seaside resort on the south coast of England where the young Mahatma Gandhi spent time during his first year as a student in Britain, and where a well-meaning widow's attempt at matchmaking led to a moral crisis resolved by confessing the truth.

memes.label: Broach
memes.description: A city on the banks of the Narmada River in Gujarat where Gandhi presided at the 1917 Broach Educational Conference and met Gangabehn Majmundar, the remarkable widow whose tireless search for the spinning wheel launched the khadi movement in practical terms.

memes.label: Burdwan
memes.description: Railway town in West Bengal where Charles Freer Andrews accompanied Mahatma Gandhi and where Gandhi encountered the hardships of third-class railway travel in India.

memes.label: Calcutta
memes.description: Major Indian city where Gandhi attended sessions of the Indian National Congress and met key political figures who shaped his entry into Indian politics

memes.label: Gandhi Called to the Bar
memes.description: Mohandas Gandhi was called to the Bar at the Inner Temple on June 10, 1891, completing his legal studies in England after three years of keeping terms and passing examinations in Roman Law and Common Law.

memes.label: Celibacy
memes.description: Gandhi's understanding of celibacy (brahmacharya) as the voluntary renunciation of sexual activity — a demanding spiritual discipline he considered essential for the pursuit of Truth and the foundation of genuine public service.

memes.label: C. F. Andrews
memes.description: British Anglican priest, missionary, and social reformer who became one of Gandhi's closest friends — known as "Deenabandhu" (Friend of the Poor) for his dedication to India's oppressed

memes.description: "The distinction between the legal practice in Natal and that in the Transvaal was that in Natal there was a joint bar..."

memes.label: "Chapter 124: XLVII: How a Client Was Saved"
memes.description: "The reader, by now, will be quite familiar with Parsi Rustomji’s name. He was one who became at once my client and co..."

memes.label: "Chapter 125: I: The First Experience"
memes.description: "Before I reached home, the party which had started from Phoenix had already arrived. According to our original plan I..."

memes.label: "Chapter 126: II: With Gokhale in Poona"
memes.description: "The moment I reached Bombay Gokhale sent me word that the Governor was desirous of seeing me, and that it might be pr..."

memes.label: "Chapter 127: III: Was It a Threat?"
memes.description: "From Poona I went to Rajkot and Porbandar, where I had to meet my brother’s widow and other relatives."

memes.label: "Chapter 128: IV: Shantiniketan"
memes.description: "From Rajkot I proceeded to Shantiniketan. The teachers and students overwhelmed me with affection. The reception was ..."

memes.label: "Chapter 129: V: Woes of Third-Class Passengers"
memes.description: "At Burdwan we came face to face with the hardships that a third-class passenger has to go through even in securing hi..."

memes.label: "Chapter 130: VI: Wooing"
memes.description: "On arrival in Poona, we found ourselves, after the performance of the shraddha ceremonies, discussing the future of t..."

memes.label: "Chapter 131: VII: Kumbha Mela"
memes.description: "I next went to Rangoon to meet Dr. Mehta, and on my way I halted at Calcutta. I was the guest of the late Babu Bhupen..."

memes.label: "Chapter 132: VIII: Lakshman Jhula"
memes.description: "It was a positive relief to reach the Gurukul and meet Mahatma Munshiramji with his giant frame. I at once felt the w..."

memes.label: "Chapter 133: IX: Founding of the Ashram"
memes.description: "The pilgrimage to the Kumbha fair was my second visit to Hardvar."

memes.label: "Chapter 134: X: On the Anvil"
memes.description: "The Ashram had been in existence only a few months when we were put to a test such as I had scarcely expected. I rece..."

memes.label: "Chapter 135: XI: Abolition of Indentured Emigration"
memes.description: "We shall, for a moment, take leave of the Ashram, which in the very beginning had to weather internal and external st..."

memes.label: "Chapter 136: XII: The Stain of Indigo"
memes.description: "Champaran is the land of King Janaka. Just as it abounds in mango groves, so used it to be full of indigo plantations..."

memes.label: "Chapter 137: XIII: The Gentle Bihari"
memes.description: "I knew Maulana Mazharul Haq in London when he was studying for the bar, and when I met him at the Bombay Congress in ..."

memes.label: "Chapter 138: XIV: Face to Face with Ahimsa"
memes.description: "My object was to inquire into the condition of the Champaran agriculturists and understand their grievances against t..."

memes.label: "Chapter 139: XV: Case Withdrawn"
memes.description: "The trial began. The Government pleader, the Magistrate and other officials were on tenterhooks. They were at a loss ..."

memes.label: "Chapter 140: XVI: Methods of Work"
memes.description: "To give a full account of the Champaran inquiry would be to narrate the history, for the period of the Champaran ryot..."

memes.label: "Chapter 141: XVII: Companions"
memes.description: "Brajkishorebabu and Rajendrababu were a matchless pair. Their devotion made it impossible for me to take a single ste..."

memes.label: "Chapter 142: XVIII: Penetrating the Villages"
memes.description: "As far as was possible we placed each school in charge of one man and one woman. These volunteers had to look after m..."

memes.label: "Chapter 143: XIX: When a Governor Is Good"
memes.description: "Whilst on the one hand social service work of the kind I have described in the foregoing chapters was being carried o..."

memes.label: "Chapter 144: XX: In Touch with Labour"
memes.description: "Whilst I was yet winding up my work on the Committee, I received a letter from Sjts. Mohanlal Pandya and Shankarlal P..."

memes.label: "Chapter 145: XXI: A Peep Into the Ashram"
memes.description: "Before I proceed to describe the progress of the labour dispute it is essential to have a peep into the Ashram. All t..."

memes.label: "Chapter 146: XXII: The Fast"
memes.description: "For the first two weeks the mill-hands exhibited great courage and self-restraint and daily held monster meetings. On..."

memes.label: "Chapter 147: XXIII: The Kheda Satyagraha"
memes.description: "No breathing time was, however, in store for me. Hardly was the Ahmedabad mill-hands’ strike over, when I had to plun..."

memes.label: "Chapter 148: XXIV: “The Onion Thief”"
memes.description: "Champaran being in a far away corner of India, and the press having been kept out of the campaign, it did not attract..."

memes.label: "Chapter 149: XXV: End of Kheda Satyagraha"
memes.description: "The campaign came to an unexpected end. It was clear that the people were exhausted, and I hesitated to let the unben..."

memes.label: "Chapter 150: XXVI: Passion for Unity"
memes.description: "The Kheda campaign was launched while the deadly war in Europe was still going on. Now a crisis had arrived, and the ..."

memes.label: "Chapter 151: XXVII: Recruiting Campaign"
memes.description: "So I attended the conference. The Viceroy was very keen on my supporting the resolution about recruiting. I asked for..."

memes.label: "Chapter 152: XXVIII: Near Death’s Door"
memes.description: "I very nearly ruined my constitution during the recruiting campaign. In those days my food principally consisted of g..."

memes.label: "Chapter 153: XXIX: The Rowlatt Bills and My Dilemma"
memes.description: "Friends and doctors assured me that I should recuperate quicker by a change to Matheran, so I went there. But the wat..."

memes.label: "Chapter 154: XXX: That Wonderful Spectacle!"
memes.description: "Thus, while on the one hand the agitation against the Rowlatt Committee’s report gathered volume and intensity, on th..."

memes.label: "Chapter 155: XXXI: That Memorable Week!—I"
memes.description: "After a short tour in South India I reached Bombay, I think on the 4th April, having received a wire from Sjt. Shanka..."

memes.label: "Chapter 156: XXXII: That Memorable Week!—II"
memes.description: "So I went to the Commissioner Mr. Griffith’s office. All about the staircase leading to the office I saw soldiers arm..."

memes.label: "Chapter 157: XXXIII: “A Himalayan Miscalculation”"
memes.description: "Almost immediately after the Ahmedabad meeting I went to Nadiad. It was here that I first used the expression “Himala..."

memes.label: "Chapter 158: XXXIV: Navajivan and Young India"
memes.description: "Thus, whilst this movement for the preservation of nonviolence was making steady though slow progress on the one hand..."

memes.label: "Chapter 159: XXXV: In the Punjab"
memes.description: "Sir Michael O’Dwyer held me responsible for all that had happened in the Punjab, and some irate young Punjabis held m..."

memes.label: "Chapter 160: XXXVI: The Khilafat Against Cow Protection?"
memes.description: "We must now leave, for the time being, these dark happenings in the Punjab."

memes.label: "Chapter 161: XXXVII: The Amritsar Congress"
memes.description: "The Punjab Government could not keep in confinement the hundreds of Punjabis who, under the martial law regime, had b..."

memes.label: "Chapter 162: XXXVIII: Congress Initiation"
memes.description: "I must regard my participation in Congress proceedings at Amritsar as my real entrance into the Congress politics. My..."

memes.label: "Chapter 163: XXXIX: The Birth of Khadi"
memes.description: "I do not remember to have seen a handloom or a spinning wheel when in 1908 I described it in Hind Swaraj as the panac..."

memes.label: "Chapter 164: XL: Found at Last!"
memes.description: "At last, after no end of wandering in Gujarat, Gangabehn found the spinning wheel in Vijapur in the Baroda State. Qui..."

memes.label: "Chapter 165: XLI: An Instructive Dialogue"
memes.description: "From its very inception the khadi movement, Swadeshi movement as it was then called, evoked much criticism from the m..."

memes.label: "Chapter 166: XLII: Its Rising Tide"
memes.description: "I must not devote any more chapters here to a description of the further progress of khadi. It would be outside the s..."

memes.label: "Chapter 167: XLIII: At Nagpur"
memes.description: "The resolutions adopted at the Calcutta special session of the Congress were to be confirmed at its annual session at..."

memes.label: Champaran
memes.description: District in Bihar where Gandhi conducted his first major satyagraha in India (1917), fighting for the rights of indigo farmers exploited by British planters

memes.label: Champaran Satyagraha
memes.description: Gandhi's first major satyagraha in India (1917), fighting the exploitation of indigo farmers in Bihar under the tinkathia system — a landmark victory for nonviolent resistance on Indian soil

memes.label: Charlestown
memes.description: Small town in Natal, South Africa — the railway terminus where Gandhi arrived after being ejected from the train at Maritzburg, and where he faced further discrimination from a stagecoach agent

memes.label: Civil Disobedience
memes.description: The deliberate, nonviolent violation of unjust laws as a form of moral protest — a key tactic within Gandhi's broader satyagraha framework

memes.label: "Colophon"
memes.description: "# Colophon"

memes.label: Compassion
memes.description: Gandhi's understanding of compassion as active identification with the suffering of others — the emotional foundation of ahimsa and the test of genuine religious conviction, extending beyond humanity to all living beings.

memes.label: Courage
memes.description: Gandhi's understanding of courage — not as the absence of fear but as the capacity to act rightly despite fear, cultivated through truth-telling and tested in public life.

memes.label: Dada Abdulla
memes.description: Gujarati merchant in South Africa whose commercial dispute brought Gandhi to Durban in 1893, inadvertently launching his career in South Africa

memes.label: Delhi
memes.description: The imperial capital of British India where Gandhi attended the Viceroy's War Conference in 1918 and where the 1919 hartal against the Rowlatt Act demonstrated unprecedented Hindu-Muslim unity

memes.label: Gandhi's First Departure for England
memes.description: Mohandas Gandhi's first overseas journey, departing from Bombay on September 4, 1888, to study law in England, overcoming caste opposition, family hesitation, and his own crippling shyness.

memes.label: Detachment
memes.description: Gandhi's understanding of detachment — not as indifference or withdrawal from the world but as the capacity to act fully in the world while remaining inwardly free from attachment to outcomes, rooted in the Gita's teaching of nishkama karma.

memes.label: Diet
memes.description: Gandhi's lifelong experiments with food and diet — from his vow of vegetarianism to radical dietary simplification as a tool of self-discipline and spiritual practice.

memes.label: Discipline
memes.description: Discipline — both self-discipline and communal discipline — as a foundational practice in Gandhi's spiritual life, extending from personal vows to the training of children and the organization of community action.

memes.label: Dr. Joseph Booth
memes.description: English clergyman in South Africa who was one of Gandhi's early mentors — helped him navigate Christian theology while deepening his commitment to interfaith understanding

memes.label: Durban
memes.description: Port city in Natal, South Africa — Gandhi's first home in Africa (1893–1914), where he established his legal practice and the Phoenix Settlement

memes.label: Durban Farewell Meeting
memes.description: Farewell gathering in 1894 at Sydenham where Gandhi first learned of the Indian Franchise Bill and was persuaded by the Indian merchants of Durban to cancel his return to India and lead their political resistance — the founding moment of his public career in South Africa.

memes.label: Duty
memes.description: The concept of moral obligation and righteous action as explored in Gandhi's autobiography.

memes.label: Empire Theatre Meeting
memes.description: The historic mass meeting of September 11, 1906, at Johannesburg's Empire Theatre, where the Indian community took a solemn oath to defy the registration law — marking the birth of organized satyagraha

memes.label: "Endnotes"
memes.description: "# Endnotes"

memes.label: England
memes.description: The country where Gandhi trained as a barrister from 1888 to 1891 — his first extended stay outside India and a formative period in his personal and professional development

memes.label: Equality
memes.description: Gandhi's understanding of equality — from equal partnership in marriage to the spiritual ideal of seeing all beings with equal regard, rooted in the Gita's teaching of samabhava.

memes.label: Faith
memes.description: Gandhi's evolving understanding of faith — from childhood ritual to living trust in God, nurtured by the Ramanama seed sown by his nurse Rambha and tested through public trials.

memes.label: "Farewell"
memes.description: "My life from this point onward has been so public that there is hardly anything about it that people do not know. Mor..."

memes.label: Fasting
memes.description: The practice of abstaining from food as a means of spiritual purification, self-discipline, and — in Gandhi's application — moral and political pressure

memes.label: Fearlessness
memes.description: The quality of being free from fear — one of the eleven vows of Gandhi's ashram, a trait he admired in his coworkers, and a precondition he identified for genuine nonviolent action.

memes.label: First Class Train Incident
memes.description: The night in 1893 when Gandhi, holding a valid first-class ticket, was thrown off the train at Maritzburg station in South Africa — the catalytic experience of racial injustice that launched his lifelong fight against colour prejudice

memes.label: Forgiveness
memes.description: Gandhi's understanding of forgiveness as both a personal discipline and a public practice — the capacity to forgive oneself and others as essential to nonviolent living.

memes.label: Foundation of Phoenix Settlement
memes.description: The establishment of Gandhi's first ashram in 1904 near Durban, inspired by Ruskin's Unto This Last — the model for all his later intentional communities

memes.label: Founding of Satyagraha Ashram
memes.description: The formal establishment of the Satyagraha Ashram on May 25, 1915, at Kochrab bungalow in Ahmedabad, marking Gandhi's first institutional foundation in India after his return from South Africa.

memes.label: Gandhi's Brahmacharya Vow
memes.description: Gandhi's formal adoption of the brahmacharya (celibacy) vow in 1906 at the Phoenix Settlement, a landmark decision in his spiritual evolution that preceded the birth of satyagraha.

memes.label: Death of Karamchand Gandhi
memes.description: The death of Gandhi's father Karamchand Gandhi in 1885, an event that Gandhi later described as a source of lasting shame due to his absence at the final moment.
aliases: My Father's Death and My Double Shame

memes.label: Gandhi's Child Marriage
memes.description: Gandhi's marriage at the age of thirteen in Porbandar, a triple wedding ceremony arranged by his family.

memes.label: Gandhi Meets Gopal Krishna Gokhale
memes.description: Gandhi's first meeting with Gopal Krishna Gokhale at Fergusson College in Poona, which marked the beginning of one of the most important mentor relationships in Gandhi's political life.

memes.label: Gandhi's Vegetarian Experiment in London
memes.description: Gandhi's adoption and deepening commitment to vegetarianism during his student years in London, including joining the Vegetarian Society and conducting dietetic experiments.

memes.label: Gaya
memes.description: City in Bihar near the Champaran region — where "Gaya Babu," a well-known local lawyer, hosted Gandhi during his 1917 Champaran fact-finding mission

memes.label: General Jan Smuts
memes.description: South African statesman and Gandhi's primary political adversary during the satyagraha campaigns — later became a respected interlocutor

memes.label: God
memes.description: Gandhi's evolving understanding of God — from a vague concept to an immanent moral force identified with Truth, guiding his experiments and shaping his vision of self-realization.

memes.label: Gopal Krishna Gokhale
memes.description: Indian political leader, mentor to Gandhi, and moderate nationalist who guided Gandhi's early political career in India — described by Gandhi as his "political guru"

memes.label: Government of India
memes.description: The British colonial administration that governed India — simultaneously the target of Gandhi's campaigns and the institutional framework within which his experiments in nonviolent resistance operated.

memes.label: Gujarat
memes.description: Western Indian region, Gandhi's homeland and the epicenter of his early Indian political work including the Kheda Satyagraha.

memes.label: Hardvar
memes.description: Ancient pilgrimage city on the Ganges where Gandhi attended the 1915 Kumbh Mela and had formative experiences with sanitation work and self-discipline.

memes.label: Henry Polak
memes.description: British-born journalist and close associate of Gandhi in South Africa — sub-editor of Indian Opinion and a key supporter during the satyagraha campaigns

memes.label: Hermann Kallenbach
memes.description: German-Jewish architect and one of Gandhi's closest friends in South Africa — co-founder of Tolstoy Farm and devoted supporter of the satyagraha movement

memes.label: Home Rule Movement
memes.description: The Indian Home Rule agitation led by Dr. Annie Besant and the Indian National Congress during World War I, advocating for self-government within the British Empire.

memes.label: Hrishikesh
memes.description: Pilgrimage town on the Ganges — Gandhi visited after the Hardvar Kumbh Mela, walking to the Lakshman Jhula bridge and reflecting on sacred symbols, religious symbolism, and the desecration of holy places

memes.label: Humility
memes.description: The virtue of self-effacement and freedom from pride that Gandhi considered essential for the satyagrahi — the capacity to recognize one's own fallibility and to see opponents as potential teachers

memes.label: Indenture System
memes.description: The system of contract labor that brought tens of thousands of Indians to South Africa — the population whose rights Gandhi fought to defend and whose suffering shaped his political awakening.

memes.label: India
memes.description: India as it appears in Gandhi's autobiography — the homeland he left and returned to, the stage for his political awakening, and the ultimate object of his campaign for Swaraj.

memes.label: Indian National Congress
memes.description: The principal political organization of India's independence movement, which Gandhi transformed from an elite debating society into a mass movement

memes.label: Indian Opinion
memes.description: The weekly newspaper founded by Gandhi in 1903 in Durban that served as the voice of the Indian community in South Africa and the laboratory for Gandhi's ideas on journalism, simplicity, and public service.

memes.label: Inner Voice
memes.description: Gandhi's concept of the inner voice — the still, small voice of conscience that he regarded as the ultimate guide in moral decisions, cultivated through silence, prayer, and the discipline of truth.

memes.label: "Introduction"
memes.description: "But a God-fearing friend had his doubts, which he shared with me on my day of silence. “What has set you on this adve..."

memes.label: Jallianwala Bagh
memes.description: Public garden in Amritsar, Punjab — site of the 1919 massacre that shocked India and became the focus of Gandhi's memorial fundraising efforts for the Congress

memes.label: Jallianwala Bagh Massacre
memes.description: The massacre of unarmed Indian civilians by British troops under General Dyer at Amritsar on April 13, 1919 — an atrocity that shattered Indian faith in British justice and galvanized the independence movement

memes.label: Jamnagar
memes.description: Princely state city in the Kathiawad region of Gujarat where Gandhi argued his first significant legal case upon returning from England.

memes.label: Johannesburg
memes.description: The South African city where Gandhi built his most successful legal practice and where organized satyagraha was born at the Empire Theatre in 1906

memes.label: Junagadh
memes.description: Princely state in Kathiawad, Gujarat — where Gandhi's grandfather Uttamchand Gandhi served as Diwan after being compelled to leave Porbandar, and the origin of the Junagadh vakil who accompanied young Gandhi to England

memes.label: Karachi
memes.description: Port city in Sindh (now in Pakistan) — one of the stops on Gandhi's 1896 India tour to mobilize public support for Indians in South Africa

memes.label: Karamchand Gandhi
memes.description: Gandhi's father, a diwan (chief minister) of Porbandar, Rajkot, and Vankaner, whose integrity and devotion to duty left a lasting impression on his youngest son

memes.label: Kasturba Gandhi
memes.description: Gandhi's wife and partner in his experiments — married at age thirteen, she shared his political struggles, imprisonments, and spiritual disciplines

memes.label: Kathiawad
memes.description: Peninsula region in Gujarat — Gandhi's birthplace and ancestral home, the cradle of his early life, education, and first encounters with Indian political intrigue

memes.label: Khadi
memes.description: Hand-spun and hand-woven Indian cloth that Gandhi made the material symbol of India's struggle for self-reliance and the centerpiece of his constructive programme

memes.label: Kheda
memes.description: District in Gujarat where Gandhi led the 1918 Kheda Satyagraha — a nonviolent peasant struggle for revenue suspension after widespread crop failure

memes.label: Kheda Satyagraha
memes.description: Gandhi's 1918 campaign in Gujarat's Kheda district, where peasants facing crop failure refused to pay land revenue — a landmark in nonviolent tax resistance

memes.label: Khilafat Movement
memes.description: The pan-Islamic protest movement (1919–1924) against the dismemberment of the Ottoman Caliphate, which Gandhi championed as an opportunity for Hindu-Muslim unity and which became intertwined with the Non-cooperation Movement.

memes.label: Kumbh Mela
memes.description: The massive Hindu pilgrimage festival at Hardvar in 1915, attended by Gandhi on his return to India — an experience that prompted him to adopt lifelong dietary vows and shaped his understanding of popular faith

memes.label: Lahore
memes.description: Major city in Punjab (now Pakistan) — site of mass demonstrations during the Rowlatt Satyagraha, where Gandhi witnessed an overwhelming public reception

memes.label: Lakshmidas Gandhi
memes.description: Gandhi's elder brother — the first in the family to fall under the influence of the friend who led Gandhi toward meat-eating, and a figure whose financial struggles shaped Gandhi's sense of family responsibility and later his radical decision to renounce personal wealth for community service.

memes.label: Leo Tolstoy
memes.description: Russian writer and Christian anarchist whose writings on nonviolence and civil disobedience profoundly influenced Gandhi's development of satyagraha

memes.label: London
memes.description: Capital of England where Gandhi studied law from 1888 to 1891 — the site of his vegetarian awakening and first sustained encounter with Western civilization

memes.label: Lord Chelmsford
memes.description: Viceroy of India (1916–1921) who maintained cordial relations with Gandhi, removed the Viramgam customs cordon, and engaged Gandhi in the war conference deliberations during World War I

memes.label: Lord Curzon
memes.description: Viceroy of India (1899–1905) whose partition of Bengal in 1905 sparked the Swadeshi movement and whose durbar occasioned Gandhi's reflections on Indian subservience

memes.label: Lucknow
memes.description: Capital city of Uttar Pradesh, India, where Gandhi attended the 1916 Congress session and met Rajkumar Shukla, the tenant farmer who persuaded him to investigate conditions in Champaran.

memes.label: Madras
memes.description: Major South Indian city (now Chennai) where Gandhi found enthusiastic support for the South African Indian cause during his 1896–1901 advocacy tour.

memes.label: Maganlal Gandhi
memes.description: Gandhi's cousin and trusted lieutenant who managed the Phoenix Settlement in South Africa during Gandhi's absences

memes.label: Mahatma Gandhi
memes.description: The author of the autobiography, leader of India's nonviolent independence movement, and the central figure around whom all other persons, events, and concepts in this wiki are organized

memes.label: Manilal Gandhi
memes.description: Gandhi's second son, whose childhood illness and recovery through natural healing methods is vividly narrated in the autobiography.

memes.label: Mansukhlal Naazar
memes.description: The editor of Indian Opinion during its early years at Phoenix Settlement — a capable journalist who remained in Durban to direct the paper while Gandhi and others experimented with communal living and manual labour.

memes.label: Manual Labour
memes.description: Gandhi's conviction that every person must perform some form of manual labour for their livelihood — a principle learned from Tolstoy and Ruskin, practiced at Phoenix Settlement, and central to his vision of a just and self-reliant society.

memes.label: Maritzburg
memes.description: City in Natal, South Africa, where Gandhi was thrown off a train in 1893 — the defining moment that launched his fight against racial discrimination.

memes.label: Mavji Dave
memes.description: Elderly Brahmin friend of the Gandhi family who advised Gandhi's father and later counseled the young Gandhi — an early influence on his moral development

memes.label: Morality
memes.description: Gandhi's conviction that morality is the foundation of all things and the essence of religion.

memes.label: Motihari
memes.description: Town in Bihar's Champaran district where Gandhi was summoned to court in 1917, marking the formal beginning of the Champaran Satyagraha.

memes.label: Pandit Motilal Nehru
memes.description: Eminent lawyer and Congress leader from Allahabad, father of Jawaharlal Nehru, who was among the first senior leaders to support Gandhi's noncooperation resolution in 1920.

memes.label: Mozambique
memes.description: Portuguese colony on the east coast of Africa where Gandhi's ship made port on his first voyage to South Africa in 1893.

memes.label: Muzaffarpur
memes.description: City in Bihar — Gandhi's first stop on his way to Champaran in 1917, where he was hosted by Professor Kripalani and Professor Malkani

memes.label: Nadiad
memes.description: Town in Gujarat's Kheda district that served as Gandhi's headquarters during the 1918 Kheda Satyagraha and the 1918 recruitment campaign.

memes.label: Nagpur
memes.description: City in Maharashtra, India — site of the 1920 Congress session where Gandhi's non-cooperation resolution and constitutional reforms were adopted

memes.label: Narayan Hemchandra
memes.description: A Gujarati writer and traveler whom Gandhi befriended in London — an eccentric, penniless, and utterly fearless man whose radical simplicity and frankness provided Gandhi with a living example of a life devoted entirely to literary and spiritual pursuits.

memes.label: Natal
memes.description: South African province where Gandhi lived and worked from 1893 — home to Durban, Phoenix Settlement, and the largest Indian population in South Africa

memes.label: Natal Indian Congress
memes.description: The political organization founded by Gandhi in 1894 to advocate for Indian rights in Natal — his first experiment in political organizing

memes.label: Non-Cooperation
memes.description: Gandhi's strategy of nonviolent non-cooperation — the withdrawal of consent and participation from an unjust system as a moral and political weapon, first articulated at the 1919 Khilafat Conference and later applied on a mass scale.

memes.label: Non-cooperation Movement
memes.description: Gandhi's 1920–22 nationwide campaign of nonviolent non-cooperation with British rule — the first mass independence movement in Indian history

memes.label: Non-Possession
memes.description: Gandhi's understanding of non-possession (aparigraha) — the voluntary renunciation of property and wealth as a spiritual discipline, learned from the Bhagavad Gita and practiced as the foundation of his economic philosophy.

memes.label: Nonviolence
memes.description: Gandhi's principle of nonviolence as a positive force — not mere abstention from physical harm but active love and forgiveness, the foundation of satyagraha and his entire moral-political philosophy.

memes.label: Paris
memes.description: Capital of France — Gandhi visited in 1890 for the Great Exhibition and was deeply moved by its ancient churches, contrasting their spiritual grandeur with the city's worldly fashions

memes.label: Passive Resistance
memes.description: The term "passive resistance" was the original English name for what Gandhi would later rename Satyagraha — a method of nonviolent confrontation that he distinguished sharply from the weakness and potential violence he saw in conventional "passive resistance."

memes.label: Patna
memes.description: Capital city of Bihar where Gandhi stopped en route to Champaran in 1917, hosted by Rajendra Prasad.

memes.label: Perseverance
memes.description: The quality of persistent effort and steadfastness that Gandhi cultivated in himself, admired in others, and regarded as essential to both spiritual growth and practical achievement.

memes.label: Pherozeshah Mehta
memes.description: The preeminent lawyer and Congress leader of Bombay, known as "the Lion of Bombay," whose commanding presence and political acumen left a lasting impression on the young Gandhi.

memes.label: Phoenix Settlement
memes.description: Gandhi's first ashram, established in 1904 near Durban — home to Indian Opinion and the first community organized around Gandhian principles of simple living

memes.label: Poona
memes.description: City in Maharashtra that served as Gokhale's base and the headquarters of the Servants of India Society — a crucial waypoint in Gandhi's political education upon returning from South Africa

memes.label: Porbandar
memes.description: Coastal city in Gujarat, western India — birthplace of Mahatma Gandhi on October 2, 1869, and seat of the Gandhi family's ancestral home

memes.label: Portsmouth
memes.description: English seaport — Gandhi attended a Vegetarian Conference here in 1890 and faced a moral trial that taught him the importance of vigilance over his vows

memes.label: Prayer
memes.description: The practice of prayer as a source of inner strength, moral guidance, and communion with Truth — central to Gandhi's daily spiritual discipline

memes.label: Pretoria
memes.description: Capital of the Transvaal where Gandhi spent his first year in South Africa — the city where he experienced racial discrimination and began his transformation

memes.label: Punjab
memes.description: North-western province of British India where the 1919 martial law and Jallianwala Bagh massacre became a turning point in Gandhi's relationship with the British Empire

memes.label: Punjab Disturbances of 1919
memes.description: The wave of protests, British martial law, and systemic repression in the Punjab following the Rowlatt Act agitation of 1919 — including the Jallianwala Bagh massacre, the crawling order, and the Congress-led independent inquiry that documented British atrocities

memes.label: Purity
memes.description: The ideal of purity — of heart, conduct, and diet — as a recurring theme in Gandhi's spiritual experiments and the foundation for his political practice of satyagraha.

memes.label: Putlibai Gandhi
memes.description: Gandhi's mother — a deeply religious woman whose saintliness, fasting discipline, and moral example left an enduring imprint on Gandhi's character and spiritual aspirations.

memes.label: Rajkot
memes.description: City in Gujarat where Gandhi spent most of his childhood and early education — his father served as Diwan of Rajkot after leaving Porbandar

memes.label: Rangoon
memes.description: Capital of Burma (now Yangon, Myanmar) visited by Gandhi in 1902, where he encountered severe plague conditions and Indian labourers working in harsh circumstances.

memes.label: Raychandbhai
memes.description: Jain poet, spiritual seeker, and Gandhi's spiritual mentor — one of the three most important influences on Gandhi's life, alongside Tolstoy and Ruskin

memes.label: Religious Tolerance
memes.description: Gandhi's understanding of religious tolerance — not mere coexistence but active respect for all faiths, cultivated from childhood through his father's example of listening to diverse religious traditions.

memes.label: Renunciation
memes.description: Gandhi's understanding of renunciation — not as world-rejection but as the disciplined letting-go of desires that obstruct the pursuit of truth, a practice refined through vows and dietary experiments.

memes.label: Rev. Joseph Doke
memes.description: Baptist minister in Johannesburg who became Gandhi's close friend and wrote the first biography of Gandhi, "M. K. Gandhi: An Indian Patriot in South Africa" (1909)

memes.label: Round Table Conference
memes.description: Series of conferences in London (1930–32) between British officials and Indian leaders on constitutional reform — Gandhi attended the second conference as the sole Congress representative

memes.label: Rowlatt Act
memes.description: The repressive 1919 colonial law that extended wartime emergency powers into peacetime, triggering Gandhi's first nationwide satyagraha in India and the Jallianwala Bagh massacre

memes.label: Rowlatt Satyagraha
memes.description: Gandhi's 1919 nationwide protest against the repressive Rowlatt Acts — the first all-India satyagraha campaign, which led to the establishment of the Satyagraha Sabha

memes.label: Parsi Rustomji (Rustomji Jivanji Ghorkhodu)
memes.description: Parsi merchant in Durban who became one of Gandhi's closest clients and coworkers in the South African Indian community, involved in the Natal Indian Congress and the Phoenix Settlement.

memes.label: Sabarmati
memes.description: Location of Gandhi's Satyagraha Ashram near Ahmedabad, Gujarat, established in 1917 and serving as his principal residence and organizational headquarters in India.

memes.label: Sabarmati Ashram
memes.description: Gandhi's primary ashram in India, founded in 1915 on the banks of the Sabarmati River near Ahmedabad — the launch point for the Salt March and home to his community for over a decade

memes.label: Sacrifice
memes.description: The act of giving up personal comfort, pleasure, or interest for a higher purpose — a recurring theme in Gandhi's life, observed in communities and individuals alike, and central to his understanding of service and spiritual growth.

memes.label: Salt March
memes.description: Gandhi's iconic 1930 march to the sea to make salt in defiance of the British salt monopoly — the most famous act of civil disobedience in modern history

memes.label: Satyagraha
memes.description: Gandhi's doctrine of nonviolent resistance rooted in the pursuit of truth — literally "holding firmly to truth"

relations.label: Chapter 38: What It Is to Be a "Coolie"

memes.label: Satyagraha Sabha
memes.description: The organization established by Gandhi in 1919 at Bombay to coordinate the nationwide Rowlatt Satyagraha — a dedicated body for nonviolent resistance

memes.label: Gandhi's Second Voyage to South Africa
memes.description: In December 1896, Gandhi sailed from Bombay to Durban aboard the SS Courland with his wife and two sons, facing a violent storm at sea before arriving to confront the political storm awaiting him on land.

memes.label: Self-Control
memes.description: Gandhi's understanding of self-control as the mastery of the senses and desires — the indispensable inner discipline without which outer nonviolence and truth-seeking are impossible, culminating in the vow of brahmacharya.

memes.label: Self-Restraint
memes.description: The practice of controlling one's desires and impulses — a foundational discipline in Gandhi's personal and communal life.

memes.label: Self-Sacrifice
memes.description: Gandhi's understanding of self-sacrifice — not as self-negation but as the willing surrender of personal comfort and ambition for the service of others, a discipline inseparable from truth-seeking.

memes.label: Servants of India Society
memes.description: The organization founded by Gopal Krishna Gokhale in 1905 to train Indians for public service — a model of disciplined national service that influenced Gandhi

memes.label: Service
memes.description: Gandhi's conviction that service to the community — especially to the poor — is the practical path to self-realization and the necessary expression of a truthful life; the "religion of service" that shaped his entire public career.

memes.label: Shantiniketan
memes.description: The ashram and educational institution founded by Rabindranath Tagore in rural Bengal, where Gandhi visited in 1915 and reunited with his Phoenix party members upon returning from South Africa.

memes.label: Sheth Abdul Gani
memes.description: Johannesburg-based Indian merchant who welcomed Gandhi upon his arrival in the city and accompanied him during the historic first-class railway journey to Pretoria

memes.label: Sheth Abdul Karim Jhaveri
memes.description: Partner in Dada Abdulla and Co. who first engaged Gandhi for legal work in South Africa, setting in motion his twenty-year stay on the continent

memes.label: Sheth Ambalal Sarabhai
memes.description: Leading Ahmedabad mill-owner who opposed Gandhi during the 1918 textile strike yet became a close friend and cared for Gandhi during his severe illness

memes.label: Sheth Haji Habib
memes.description: Muslim merchant leader in South Africa who proposed the idea of a solemn satyagraha pledge at the historic Empire Theatre meeting of 1906

memes.label: Swami Shraddhanand
memes.description: Hindu reformer and Arya Samaj leader who wanted Gandhi to settle in Hardvar and establish his ashram there

memes.label: Silence
memes.description: The practice of observing periods of complete silence as a discipline of self-control, inner listening, and spiritual concentration — Gandhi observed weekly silence for decades

memes.label: Simplicity
memes.description: Gandhi's lifelong commitment to simplicity and plain living — reducing material wants, living within modest means, and finding freedom through the shedding of unnecessary possessions.

memes.label: Sir Edward Gait
memes.description: Lieutenant Governor of Bihar and Orissa who appointed the Champaran Agrarian Inquiry Committee and ensured the passage of the Champaran Agrarian Act

memes.label: Sir Edwin Arnold
memes.description: English poet and journalist whose translations of the Bhagavad Gita (The Song Celestial) and The Light of Asia deeply influenced Gandhi's spiritual development

memes.label: Sir Gurudas Banerji
memes.description: Distinguished judge of the Calcutta High Court whose suggestion that humility be added as an Ashram observance provoked Gandhi's profound reflection on the nature of humility and self-effacement

memes.label: Sir Michael O'Dwyer
memes.description: Lieutenant Governor of the Punjab (1913–1919) who ordered Gandhi's arrest at Palwal and held him responsible for the disturbances that culminated in the Jallianwala Bagh massacre

memes.label: South Africa
memes.description: The land where Gandhi spent twenty-one formative years (1893–1914), developing satyagraha in response to racial discrimination and emerging as a political and spiritual leader of the Indian diaspora.

memes.label: Southampton
memes.description: Major port city on the south coast of England where Mahatma Gandhi first arrived in Britain in 1888 as a student and later passed through in 1914 at the outbreak of World War I.

memes.label: Spion Kop
memes.description: A hill in Natal, South Africa, site of a major Boer War battle in January 1900 where the British suffered a severe repulse — and where Gandhi's Indian Ambulance Corps was asked to serve within the firing line for the first time, carrying wounded soldiers including General Woodgate.

memes.label: Standerton
memes.description: A small Transvaal village where Gandhi's stagecoach halted overnight during his first journey from Durban to Pretoria in 1893, and where local Indians came to his aid after a harrowing assault by a white passenger.

memes.label: Suffering
memes.description: The role of suffering as spiritual discipline and political witness in Gandhi's autobiography.

memes.label: Surat
memes.description: A city in Gujarat where Gandhi was handed over from one police escort to another while being forcibly deported back to Bombay after his arrest for attempting to enter the Punjab in 1919, during the tense days following the Rowlatt Act satyagraha.

memes.label: Swadeshi
memes.description: The principle of economic self-reliance through the use of locally produced goods — particularly hand-spun khadi — as both a spiritual discipline and a political strategy

memes.label: Swaraj
memes.description: The principle of self-rule — both political independence for India and individual self-mastery — that Gandhi placed at the center of his political vision

memes.label: Tolstoy Farm
memes.description: Gandhi's second South African ashram, established in 1910 near Johannesburg — named after Leo Tolstoy and home to the final phase of the South African satyagraha

memes.label: "Translator’s Preface"
memes.description: "# Translator’s Preface"

memes.label: Transvaal
memes.description: South African province where the most intense phase of Gandhi's satyagraha campaigns took place — the territory that birthed organized nonviolent resistance

memes.label: Truth
memes.description: The supreme principle of Gandhi's philosophy — for him, Truth is God, and the pursuit of truth through nonviolent means is the purpose of human life

memes.label: Truthfulness
memes.description: Gandhi's understanding of truthfulness in word as a dimension of the sovereign principle of Truth — the practice of absolute honesty in speech, which he considered the foundation of all moral life and the precondition for spiritual growth.

memes.label: Tyeb Sheth (Tyeb Haji Khan Muhammad)
memes.description: Prominent Indian merchant in Pretoria, near relative of Abdulla Sheth, who was the opposing party in Gandhi's first major legal case in South Africa.

memes.label: "Uncopyright"
memes.description: "# Uncopyright"

memes.label: Untouchability
memes.description: The practice of caste-based exclusion and pollution that Gandhi campaigned against throughout his public life, which he considered a corruption of Hinduism and a moral blot on Indian society

memes.label: Vallabhbhai Patel (Sardar Patel)
memes.description: Lawyer and Congress leader from Gujarat who became one of Gandhi's closest coworkers during the Kheda Satyagraha and the Ahmedabad mill workers' strike, later known as Sardar Patel.

memes.label: London Vegetarian Society
memes.description: The organization Gandhi joined as a law student in London — his first experience of an ethical movement and the community that transformed vegetarianism from a cultural habit into a moral conviction

memes.label: Vegetarianism
memes.description: The practice of abstaining from meat, which Gandhi adopted as a moral commitment and which served as an early training ground for his broader philosophy of self-restraint and nonviolence

memes.label: Ventnor
memes.description: A seaside resort on the Isle of Wight, England, where the young Gandhi stayed during his student years and experienced episodes of shyness that he later reflected on with characteristic candour.

memes.label: Veraval
memes.description: A port town on the Saurashtra coast of Gujarat where Gandhi attended a court hearing as a young barrister during a plague epidemic, and where his legal successes in Jamnagar and Veraval convinced Kevalram Dave he was destined for Bombay.

memes.label: Viramgam
memes.description: Town in Gujarat — site of an oppressive customs cordon that Gandhi campaigned against, becoming his first successful satyagraha intervention on Indian soil

memes.label: Viramgam Customs Cordon
memes.description: Gandhi's campaign against the oppressive customs cordon at Viramgam, where railway passengers were subjected to humiliating medical inspections and delays.

memes.label: Vow
memes.description: The practice of making binding commitments — particularly vows of self-restraint — as a source of moral strength and a shield against temptation in Gandhi's ethical system

memes.label: Wadhwan
memes.description: A town in the Kathiawad region of Gujarat where Gandhi was medically inspected during a plague outbreak on his third-class rail journey, and where the tailor Motilal — a noted public worker — met him and enlisted his help against the Viramgam customs cordon.

memes.label: Wardha
memes.description: Town in Maharashtra, India, near Gandhi's Sevagram ashram; associated with several of Gandhi's close coworkers.

memes.label: Delhi War Conference
memes.description: The 1918 war conference in Delhi convened by Viceroy Lord Chelmsford, where Gandhi agreed to support the British war effort by leading a recruiting campaign in Kheda.

memes.label: Yeravda
memes.description: Central prison near Pune, India, where Mahatma Gandhi was imprisoned multiple times and wrote the major portion of his autobiography "The Story of My Experiments with Truth."

memes.label: Zanzibar
memes.description: An East African island port where Gandhi's ship made an extended stopover during his first voyage to South Africa in 1893, giving him his first exposure to a colonial court and prompting him to begin studying law and bookkeeping.

memes.label: Zulu Rebellion
memes.description: The 1906 uprising in Natal during which Gandhi served with an ambulance corps — the experience that intensified his commitment to self-control and led to the brahmacharya vow
